mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending and economic health, which typically translates into stronger corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month percentage change in RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which initially boosts investor confidence in economic strength and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% during identified late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains stable or increases only modestly, it implies falling real interest rates. This makes equities relatively more attractive compared to fixed income, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0.25%
regime_hint: inflationary environment with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in industrial production during an economic recovery signals increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which is a positive indicator for corporate earnings and leads to short-term positive market reactions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances during identified post-recession recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in housing starts reflects strong underlying demand, consumer confidence, and investment in real estate, which broadly supports economic activity and translates into positive S&P 500 performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month percentage change in HOUST over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained deceleration in non-farm payroll employment growth, falling below a critical threshold, signals a weakening labor market and potential economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below the specified threshold during periods of economic slowdown.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of robust GDP growth and strong retail sales indicates a broad-based and healthy economic expansion. This environment supports strong corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined condition of strong GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not lead to significantly positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.1) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, any unexpected and significant increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank. This can negatively impact market sentiment and lead to immediate S&P 500 declines over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises sharply during identified high inflation regimes.
